deploy/charts/benthos-collector.purpose: Helm chart packaging the benthos-collector binary as a Kubernetes StatefulSet. Its primary constraint is enforcing stable pod identity (for leader election) and protecting credentials in Secrets rather than ConfigMaps.

patterns.name: All names via helpers
patterns.description: Every resource name must use `benthos-collector.fullname` or `benthos-collector.componentName` from _helpers.tpl — never string-concat `.Release.Name` directly.

patterns.name: Config stored in Secret
patterns.description: The Benthos config.yaml is always rendered into a Secret (secret.yaml), never a ConfigMap, to protect embedded credentials. Adding a parallel ConfigMap for the same data violates this.
patterns.example: kind: Secret — data: config.yaml: {{ .Values.config | toYaml | b64enc }}

patterns.name: Args routed through benthos-collector.args helper
patterns.description: Container startup args must come from the `benthos-collector.args` helper in _helpers.tpl, which validates config-source exclusivity (config vs configFile vs preset). Never add args inline to statefulset.yaml.

patterns.name: Downward-API env vars injected unconditionally
patterns.description: K8S_POD_NAME and K8S_NAMESPACE are injected from the Downward API in statefulset.yaml unconditionally. New env vars must not conflict with these reserved names.
patterns.example:
- name: K8S_POD_NAME
  valueFrom:
    fieldRef:
      fieldPath: metadata.name

patterns.name: Config checksum annotation triggers pod rollout
patterns.description: The pod template must carry `checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/secret.yaml") . | sha256sum }}` so config changes trigger rollouts automatically.

patterns.name: StatefulSet not Deployment
patterns.description: The workload is a StatefulSet to provide stable pod identity required by the leader-election implementation and optionally persistent storage via volumeClaimTemplates.

patterns.name: RBAC gated on rbac.create
patterns.description: RBAC resources (Role, RoleBinding) are wrapped in `{{- if .Values.rbac.create }}` so operators who manage RBAC externally can opt out.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all naming, label, and args helpers. Source of truth for benthos-collector.fullname, benthos-collector.args, and common label blocks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: benthos-collector.args validates config-source exclusivity; adding a new config source requires updating this helper, not statefulset.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Core workload definition. Mounts the Secret as a volume, sets Downward-API env vars, and references the args helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must keep checksum/config annotation; must not inline args; must not reference ConfigMap for config data.

key_file_guides.role: Renders the Benthos config.yaml as a base64-encoded Secret. Always rendered even when config is empty.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This is the only acceptable storage for Benthos config; do not create a parallel ConfigMap.

key_file_guides.role: Creates Role and RoleBinding for leader election; guarded by .Values.rbac.create.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Leader election requires lease/get/update permissions on coordination.k8s.io; missing rules will silently fail at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the three mutually exclusive config sources (config, configFile, preset) and leaderElection, storage, and RBAC toggles.
key_file_guides.watch_for: config takes precedence over configFile which takes precedence over preset — precedence is enforced in benthos-collector.args, not values.yaml.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding release or chart name strings instead of using `benthos-collector.fullname` / `benthos-collector.componentName`
Storing Benthos config.yaml in a ConfigMap — it is always a Secret to protect embedded credentials
Adding container args directly to statefulset.yaml instead of routing through the `benthos-collector.args` helper
Adding new env vars without checking for conflicts with the mandatory Downward-API set (K8S_POD_NAME, K8S_NAMESPACE)
Using a Deployment instead of StatefulSet — pod identity is required for leader election

decisions.rationale: Leader election requires stable pod identity (hostname-based); volumeClaimTemplates also needs StatefulSet for per-pod persistent storage when storage.enabled=true.

decisions.decision: Config stored in Secret, not ConfigMap
decisions.rationale: The Benthos config may embed API tokens or credentials; Secret provides base64 encoding and access-control semantics that ConfigMap does not.
StatefulSets and Deployments don't automatically restart on ConfigMap/Secret changes; the sha256sum annotation forces a rollout whenever the config Secret content changes.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new optional feature toggle that injects env vars

deploy/charts/openmeter.purpose: Helm chart deploying all OpenMeter binaries (api, balance-worker, billing-worker, sink-worker, notification-service) plus optional bundled dependencies (Kafka, ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Redis, Svix) as Kubernetes workloads sharing a single ConfigMap-mounted config.yaml and a common image tag.

patterns.name: Component names via openmeter.componentName helper
patterns.description: Every resource name must be produced by `openmeter.componentName` (or `openmeter.fullname`) from _helpers.tpl. Hardcoding `.Release.Name` directly breaks multi-release isolation.

patterns.name: Labels via componentLabels / componentSelectorLabels
patterns.description: All label blocks on Deployments and Services must use `openmeter.componentLabels` and `openmeter.componentSelectorLabels` helpers so label selectors stay consistent.

patterns.name: Checksum/config annotation on every workload pod template
patterns.description: Each Deployment's pod template must carry `checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}` so config changes trigger rollouts.

patterns.name: Single shared image tag across all OpenMeter binaries
patterns.description: All Deployment containers use `Values.image.repository` and `Values.image.tag` (defaulting to `.Chart.AppVersion`). Never introduce per-component image overrides — all binaries ship in the same image.

patterns.name: Optional infrastructure wrapped in enabled guards
patterns.description: Resources for bundled dependencies (Svix, ClickHouse, Kafka, Redis, PostgreSQL) must be wrapped in `{{- if .Values.<service>.enabled }}`. The configmap.yaml must also guard its override block for the same flag.

patterns.name: Config via single ConfigMap volume mounted at /etc/openmeter
patterns.description: All workloads mount the same ConfigMap at /etc/openmeter/config.yaml. Per-component configuration is expressed through the shared config block; no per-Deployment ConfigMaps should be created.

patterns.name: CronJobs use Forbid concurrency and Never restart policy
patterns.description: All CronJob entries in jobs.yaml must set `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` and `restartPolicy: Never` to prevent duplicate billing runs.
patterns.example:
concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
jobTemplate:
  spec:
    template:
      spec:
        restartPolicy: Never

key_file_guides.role: Defines openmeter.fullname, openmeter.componentName, openmeter.componentLabels, openmeter.componentSelectorLabels, and init-container templates. Central authority for naming and labeling.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Init container logic is defined as named templates here, not inline in deployment.yaml — extend here when adding new init containers.

key_file_guides.role: Merges .Values.config with auto-derived connection strings for Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis, PostgreSQL, and Svix based on enabled flags. Single source of runtime config for all workloads.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Values defined in .Values.config are overwritten by chart-derived values; document this in values.yaml comments when adding new override blocks.

key_file_guides.role: Renders Deployments for api, balance-worker, billing-worker, sink-worker, notification-service. Each component gets its own replicaCount from Values.<component>.replicaCount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each Deployment must have the checksum/config annotation; all use the same image tag; binary entrypoint differs per component.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CronJobs for subscriptionSync, billingCollectInvoices, and billingAdvanceInvoices. All use the shared config volume and same image.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Schedules come from .Values.jobs.<job>.schedule; must use concurrencyPolicy: Forbid to prevent parallel billing runs.

key_file_guides.role: Renders Svix server Deployment and Service, guarded by .Values.svix.enabled. Injects signing secret and DSN from Values.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses a separate image (.Values.svix.image) — Svix is the only dependency allowed to have its own image field.

key_file_guides.role: Defines replicaCount per component, all infrastructure enabled/disabled toggles, job schedules, and the config passthrough block.
key_file_guides.watch_for: config block is a passthrough — chart-derived values overwrite it at render time; warn users with a comment.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Bitnami Kafka, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Altinity ClickHouse operator as conditional subchart dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All subchart conditions use `.Values.<name>.enabled`; new subcharts must follow the same pattern and update Chart.lock.

anti_patterns: Writing app.kubernetes.io/* labels inline instead of using openmeter.componentLabels / openmeter.componentSelectorLabels helpers
Hardcoding resource names with .Release.Name directly instead of openmeter.componentName or openmeter.fullname
Adding a new Deployment without a checksum/config annotation — config changes won't trigger rollouts
Adding optional infrastructure (new self-hosted dependency) without an `{{- if .Values.<service>.enabled -}}` guard in both the resource file and configmap.yaml override block
Using different image fields per OpenMeter component — all OpenMeter binaries share Values.image.repository and Values.image.tag

decisions.decision: Single shared ConfigMap for all workloads, merged at render time
decisions.rationale: All OpenMeter binaries read the same config.yaml format; a single ConfigMap avoids config drift between components and lets the checksum annotation trigger all workloads on any config change.
Billing and subscription-sync jobs must not run concurrently — duplicate runs would produce double-billing or race conditions in invoice advancement.

decisions.decision: All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag
decisions.rationale: The mono-repo produces a single Docker image containing all binaries; per-component image overrides would allow version skew between components that share domain package types.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new OpenMeter binary worker Deployment
code_examples.code:
# In deployment.yaml, follow existing component block:
- name: {{ include "openmeter.componentName" (list . "my-worker") }}
  replicas: {{ .Values.myWorker.replicaCount }}
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}
      labels: {{- include "openmeter.componentLabels" (list . "my-worker") | nindent 8 }}
    spec:
      containers:
        - image: {{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
          args: ["my-worker"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/openmeter
# In values.yaml add: myWorker:
#   replicaCount: 1
